'''Rhys Kennedy''' (born 11 October 1994) is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} forward, most recently for the [[London Broncos]] in the [[Super League]].

He played for the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]] and [[Brisbane Broncos]] in the [[National Rugby League]] (NRL).

==Background==
Kennedy was born in [[Moruya, New South Wales]], Australia. Rhys Kennedy played his junior football with the [[Moruya Sharks]]. Kennedy is of Swiss descent.{{citation needed|date=March 2023}}

==Playing career==
Kennedy played for the [[Melbourne Storm]] in the [[NRL Under-20s|National Youth Competition]] in 2014.

In 2016, Kennedy played for the [[Illawarra Cutters]] in the [[Intrust Super Premiership NSW]] competition.

In 2017, Kennedy joined foundation club [[North Sydney Bears|North Sydney]] who were the feeder club side for [[NRL]] side [[South Sydney Rabbitohs|South Sydney]]. Kennedy spent two seasons playing with Norths in the [[Intrust Super Premiership NSW]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.illawarramercury.com.au/story/5983902/kennedy-completes-rise-with-nrl-debut-for-south-sydney/|title=Kennedy completes rise with NRL debut for South Sydney|website=Illawarra Mercury}}</ref>

===South Sydney Rabbitohs===
On March 31, 2019, in round 3 of the [[2019 NRL season]], Kennedy made his [[NRL]] debut for [[South Sydney Rabbitohs|South Sydney]] against the [[Gold Coast Titans]].{{citation needed|date=December 2023}}

===Brisbane Broncos===
On June 25, Kennedy signed for the [[Brisbane Broncos]] in a deal that ran until the end of the 2020 season. The contract was later upgraded until the end of the [[2022 NRL season]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2019/07/30/kennedys-rollercoaster-ride-from-uber-driver-to-broncos-deal/|title=Kennedy's 'rollercoaster ride' from Uber driver to Broncos deal|website=NRL}}</ref> Standing 198 cm and weighing 110 kg, Kennedy was at the time, the tallest player in the Brisbane squad.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.zerotackle.com/just-in-broncos-sign-rabbitohs-prop-effective-immediately-45608/|title=Broncos sign Rabbitohs prop Kennedy effective immediately|last=Cotton|first=Ben|date=2019-06-24|website=Zero Tackle|language=en-US|access-date=2019-12-14}}</ref>

===Hull KR===
On 12 August 2023, Kennedy played for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in their 17-16 golden point extra-time loss to [[Leigh Leopards|Leigh]] in the Challenge Cup final.

Kennedy played a total of 26 games for Hull Kingston Rovers in the 2023 Super League season as the club finished fourth on the table and qualified for the playoffs.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66463438|title=Challenge Cup final: Hull KR 16-17 Leigh Leopards - Leigh claim first Wembley final win in 52 years|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

===London Broncos===
On 10 Nov 2023 it was reported that he had signed for the [[London Broncos]] in the [[Super League]].<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=10 Nov 2023|title=Rhys Kennedy: London Broncos sign Hull Kingston Rovers prop|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/67382276}}</ref>
